relations.note: Coral's restricted, sparing tertiary role is a specific application of the overall primary>secondary>tertiary hierarchy.
Chart emphasis reservation specializes the general dominance/accent hierarchy for chart contexts.

quotes: blues and golds dominate; tertiary colors are accents and surfaces.

rules.intent: Preserve consistent brand color weighting across all materials.
rules.rule_text: Apply the brand palette in a primary > secondary > tertiary ratio: primary/secondary blues and golds (Brand Blue #00529b, Sunshine #faa31b, Gold #ffc60a) should dominate compositions, while tertiary colors (Coral, Light Blue, Golden Sand, Graphite, White) are used only as accents and surfaces, never as the dominant palette.

quotes: Brand Blue (or White on dark/gradient backgrounds)
Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds; White on dark backgrounds
Brand Blue #00529b, underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark.
Brand Blue (or White on dark/gradient backgrounds)
Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds; White on dark backgrounds
Brand Blue #00529b, underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark.

rules.intent: Ensure legibility and brand-consistent contrast across light and dark surfaces.
rules.rule_text: Headlines, body text, and links flip color for surface contrast: headlines use Brand Blue (#00529b) on light backgrounds and switch to White on dark or gradient backgrounds; body text uses Graphite (#212121) on light backgrounds and White on dark backgrounds (with increased line height per body.line_height); links use underlined Brand Blue (#00529b) on light backgrounds and underlined White on dark backgrounds.

effect.reserved_for: emphasizing key statistical figures in charts
effect.subject: Sunshine/Gold chart emphasis color (chart.color.emphasis, e.g. #faa31b/#ffc60a)

quotes: Sunshine/Gold strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures
Sunshine/Gold strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures

rules.intent: Keep visual emphasis in data visualizations consistent and reserved for true standout figures.
rules.rule_text: Charts use Brand Blue + Sky Blue as the dominant palette (chart.color.dominant), with Sunshine/Gold strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures (chart.color.emphasis), and gray reserved for the placebo series (chart.color.placebo).

quotes: Light Blue at 70% tint (data callouts); Golden Sand at 30% tint (text-heavy callouts).
Light Blue at 70% tint (data callouts); Golden Sand at 30% tint (text-heavy callouts).

rules.intent: Differentiate callout surfaces consistently by content purpose.
rules.rule_text: Data callouts use Light Blue at a 70% tint (#E6F0F9 @70%), and text-heavy callouts use Golden Sand at a 30% tint (#FEF1C8 @30%).

quotes: sparing tertiary accent only — never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data
sparing tertiary accent only — never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data
avoid Coral in email sections unless the brief calls for it

rules.intent: Prevent Coral from being misapplied to high-visibility or functional elements it isn't approved for.
rules.rule_text: Coral (#F26A38) is a sparing tertiary accent only and must never be used for primary CTAs, headlines, or data; more generally, avoid Coral in email sections unless the creative brief specifically calls for it.

quotes: 70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a must be maintained in all instances
70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a must be maintained in all instances

rules.intent: Preserve exact, consistent logo mark rendering across all uses.
rules.rule_text: The logo icon's 70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a (Gold) must be maintained in every instance, including when the mark is used as a standalone logo or repeated as a pattern/graphic.
